notes_for_layer3: Lecture has natural sections: (1) presentation logistics + beverage containers + European cars [00:02–06:27]; (2) recap of steels and hardness trio [06:27–11:40]; (3) HY-130 / 4340 America's Cup case [11:40–13:06]; (4) Tom's welding biography and reaction-time digression [13:06–17:44]; (5) why many steels exist and the manganese/sulfur story [17:44–27:20]; (6) stainless steel genealogy: austenitic, sulfurized (303), duplex, precipitation-hardening, Mn-substituted, Hadfield's, martensitic, FCC/BCC/BCT taxonomy [27:20–37:21]; (7) hydrogen embrittlement and Mo for chloride resistance: Fenway Frank case [37:21–42:05]; (8) galvanic series, active vs passive, Aquarium case, GW Bridge anecdote [42:05–51:15]; (9) L-grades and AOD steelmaking [51:15–end]. Recommend 8–9 sections.
